scope: `sworn doctor` declares which Baton schema versions this binary grades — a graded-schema-version manifest ($id/version/GRADED-or-ADVISORY per vendored schema, including the newly-vendored contracts-v1/assembly-proof-v1 as advisory) plus a skew check, so a future protocol/runner skew is a visible WARN at doctor time, not a silent behaviour gap.

test_results.command: gofmt -l internal/baton/manifest.go internal/baton/manifest_stub.go internal/baton/manifest_test.go cmd/sworn/doctor.go cmd/sworn/doctor_test.go
test_results.output: empty — all 5 touched/new .go files gofmt-clean; the project's newline-eating-edit hazard grep ('//.*\t+(return|sendRequest|[a-z]+\()') found no matches
47 packages ok (1 no-test-files: internal/verdict, pre-existing/unrelated), 0 FAIL, zero regressions in any untouched package

reachability.evidence: TestDoctorSchemaManifestRendersGradedAndAdvisory and TestDoctorSchemaSkewFiresOnFixture (cmd/sworn/doctor_test.go) drive cmdDoctor — the exact CLI entry point `sworn doctor` invokes (Rule 1), not the leaf baton.SchemaManifest()/SchemaSkew() functions in isolation (those get separate leaf-level coverage in internal/baton/manifest_test.go, in addition per Rule 1, not as the sole proof of life). The first test asserts Group 1b renders all 7 graded schemas as [OK] status=GRADED and both contracts-v1/assembly-proof-v1 as [OK] status=ADVISORY, plus baton/schema-skew as [OK] on the real, unskewed vendored set. The second injects a made-up-v1 fixture via baton.SetSchemaMapForTest and asserts baton/schema-skew renders [WARN] (never [OK]) and the unclassified entry itself renders [WARN] status=UNCLASSIFIED, and that no [ERROR] appears anywhere (skew must not gate the exit code, D2). Manual smoke: ran `go run ./cmd/sworn doctor` live against this repo — Group 1b prints all 9 schemas with $id/version/status exactly as designed (see journal.md for the full captured output), and `baton/schema-skew` shows [OK]. Confirmed no regression to existing doctor groups: diffed the full [ERROR] line set of the CLI's stdout before vs. after this slice's changes (via `git stash` of only the S15 files) — byte-identical 75 pre-existing [ERROR] lines both times, from unrelated repo-state staleness (future-dated status.json timestamps + stale index.md render drift in OTHER in-flight releases, not introduced by and not in scope for S15).

delivered.item: AC-01: `sworn doctor`'s new Group 1b prints each vendored baton schema's $id, version, and graded-vs-advisory status; contracts-v1 and assembly-proof-v1 render ADVISORY, the other 7 (slice-status-v1, board-v1, spec-v1, proof-v1, journeys-v1, attestations-v1, verifier-verdict-v1) render GRADED. Name/$id/version are always parsed from the embedded schema bytes (baton.schemaID/parseSchemaVersion) — never hand-listed — per design decision D1.

AC-02: a deliberately-skewed schema-map fixture (one extra vendored schema absent from the graded/advisory classification table) makes doctor's skew check fire — a clearly-flagged [WARN], never a silent [OK] — for both the schema-skew line and the unclassified entry's own line.

AC-03: go build ./... and go test ./cmd/sworn/... ./internal/baton/... pass; the new Group 1b does not regress any existing doctor group (byte-identical pre-existing [ERROR] set before/after, confirmed by git-stash A/B comparison); full go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./... stays green (47 ok, 0 FAIL).
Design decisions D1-D3 (Type-2 noted defaults) transcribed into status.json.design_decisions per the Coach's accepted review pin 1, before code was written.
